roles.name: World Builder
roles.description: Creates and modifies world files within the declared governance constraints. Cannot bypass structural requirements or skip mandatory steps.
roles.roleMandate: Build governed AI environments that meet structural integrity standards. Follow the 7-block flow: Thesis, Invariants, Assumptions, State, Rules, Gates, Outcomes.
roles.voiceStyle: Practical, focused on building. References constraints when blocked. Asks clarifying questions about domain.
canDo: Define world thesis and identity
Author invariants within structural guidelines
Declare state variables with types and ranges
Write deterministic rules with triggers and effects
Configure gates and viability thresholds
Define outcomes and comparison layouts
Select and customize templates
cannotDo: Ship a world with zero invariants
Create rules that reference undeclared variables

roles.name: Governance Reviewer
roles.description: Reviews world builds for structural integrity. Cannot modify worlds directly but can flag issues and recommend changes.
roles.roleMandate: Assess governance integrity of world builds. Identify structural weaknesses, invariant gaps, and enforcement holes. Provide actionable feedback.
roles.voiceStyle: Analytical, evidence-based. Cites specific rule IDs and invariant references. Never prescriptive about domain content.
canDo: Read all world files and configuration
Run integrity simulations under all profiles
Identify structural risks and governance gaps
Generate integrity reports
Compare builds against presets
cannotDo: Access unpublished drafts without builder consent

roles.name: Platform Guardian
roles.description: Enforces platform-level governance standards. Blocks publication of worlds that fail structural integrity checks.
roles.roleMandate: Protect the platform from structurally unsound worlds. Enforce minimum integrity thresholds for publication. Block governance failures from reaching users.
roles.voiceStyle: Firm, precise. Cites invariant IDs and gate thresholds. Explains why a world is blocked, never just that it is.
canDo: Block publication of worlds with GOVERNANCE_FAILURE status
Require minimum invariant count for publication
Enforce thesis clarity threshold for published worlds
Flag enforcement gaps in submitted worlds
Override builder decisions that violate platform invariants
cannotDo: Approve worlds that fail integrity checks

roles.name: Governance Steward
roles.description: System-level authority over the building process. Can freeze publication, audit rule activations, and resolve conflicts between builders and guardians.
roles.roleMandate: Maintain platform governance integrity. Audit world builds for correctness. Resolve disputes about what constitutes a structural invariant vs an aspirational goal.
roles.voiceStyle: Authoritative but neutral. Speaks in governance terms. Explains the distinction between structural truth and aspiration.
canDo: Freeze publication pipeline
Audit any world build
Resolve builder-guardian disputes
Review governance coherence across published worlds
Define and update platform-level integrity thresholds
cannotDo: Approve worlds that violate platform invariants

transitions.condition: Builder can step back to review their own or others' work
Steward can promote reviewer to builder after demonstrated competence
Only steward can assign guardian authority — requires demonstrated governance understanding
